message:
feat(abi): add membrane runtime_policy enforcement to SysV IPC and process_vm syscalls

Instrument all System V IPC entry points (shmget, shmctl, shmat, shmdt,
semget, semctl, semop, msgget, msgctl, msgsnd, msgrcv) and cross-process
memory operations (process_vm_readv, process_vm_writev, process_madvise)
with the membrane runtime_policy decide/observe protocol. Each wrapper now:

- Calls runtime_policy::decide() before the underlying syscall to obtain a
  policy decision (Deny/Allow/Repair) based on the API family, pointer
  validity, and payload size.
- Returns -1 with EPERM errno on Deny decisions, short-circuiting the
  syscall entirely.
- Detects null-pointer-with-nonzero-size "missing payload" patterns and
  returns EFAULT when policy repair is enabled, preventing undefined
  behavior from propagating to the kernel.
- Calls runtime_policy::observe() after every exit path with scaled cost
  metrics proportional to the operation size (using scaled_cost()).

Add two inline helper functions: policy_repair_enabled() to centralize the
heal/repair gate check, and sysvipc_missing_payload() plus
process_vm_missing_iov_payload() for null-pointer payload detection.

Add 8 new integration tests in unistd_abi_test.rs covering shmdt null
pointer, semctl invalid semid, semop/msgsnd/msgrcv null payloads, and
process_vm_readv/writev/madvise null iov scenarios, all asserting the
expected errno family (EFAULT/EINVAL/EPERM).

Update xdr_abi_test.rs to use std::f64::consts::E instead of the literal
2.718281828459045 and replace the integer-cast null sentinel with
std::ptr::dangling_mut::<c_char>() for idiomatic Rust.

Regenerate all 4 conformance snapshots reflecting the expanded symbol counts
(unistd_abi 620->633 symbols, rpc_abi module split out with 152 symbols,
glibc_internal_abi reduced from 520->368 as symbols reclassified).

Sync .beads/issues.jsonl with latest task state updates.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
